There is a conspiracy on foot to
send me to jail" vehemently de-
cared Samuel T. Crane this morn
ing in addressing the jury in his
own defense in his appeal trial on
dsorderIy conduct charge. 1.-THE
police evidence is made to order;
the officers lied and perjured them
selves. They intend to get me,
and am making the fight of my
life.


Its the police evidence, the evil
dence of the Spokane police, sher-
ifs office and the sleuths that is
the strongest against me; the civil
ians have not injured me a bit
they told the truth-it's the manu-
tured stories of the police that are
depended upon to convict me.


DO you think. gentlemen of the
jury, that if | had been SO crazy as
to make the declaration, 4 can lick
any man on the police force or in
the sheriff's office that no civil
ians could be secured TO testify to
that. One officer stood 60 feet
away and yet he testified that he
clearly heard me make that state
ment. If he could hear me, there
were over 200 citizens nearer than
he and surely among them a few
